title: arrhenatherum album
intro: Arrhenatherum album, also known as oak meadow grass or white oat grass, is a perennial grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and promoting wound healing. Arrhenatherum album can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
